prompt: Draft a detailed in vitro reconstitution protocol for PCNA-dependent base-excision repair (BER) using purified recombinant human proteins, to evaluate the effect of PCNA-interacting inhibitors on DNA repair. The protocol should include: (1) purified protein preparation list: PCNA (trimer), APE1, pol β, XRCC1, LigIII, RFC clamp loader — sources and concentrations, (2) DNA substrate: 60-mer double-stranded oligonucleotide with a site-specific uracil lesion (U:G mismatch) at position 30, 5'-[32P]-labelled, (3) reaction assembly: 50 nM DNA, 100 nM PCNA, 50 nM APE1, 50 nM pol β, 50 nM LigIII/XRCC1, 50 µM dNTPs, in BER buffer (50 mM HEPES pH 7.5, 5 mM MgCl2, 1 mM DTT, 2 mM ATP), (4) inhibitor titration: add PCNA PIP-box inhibitor at 0–100 µM before assembly, (5) analysis: denaturing PAGE, phosphorimager quantification of repair product, (6) IC50 calculation by non-linear regression. Include controls: no-enzyme negative, no-inhibitor positive, and ligase-only control.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and in vitro BER reconstitution literature; target: PCNA; organism: Homo sapiens; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: DNA replication clamp in vitro base-excision repair assay
